description_long: This object is a four-panel folding screen or room divider, featuring a painted design of cherry blossom branches against a soft blue sky with a sun and distant mountains. Each panel is framed in a dark, likely wooden or metal border, and the panels are connected by visible metallic hinges, allowing the screen to be folded or expanded. The front side displays the detailed artwork, while the reverse side is plain and dark, likely to provide privacy or block light. The panels are tall and narrow, suggesting the screen is intended to stand on the floor and provide a visual or physical barrier in a room or open space. The painted surface appears to be fabric or a similar material stretched within the frames. This type of object is commonly used in urban interiors for privacy, decoration, or to divide spaces temporarily.
description: A four-panel folding screen with cherry blossom artwork, dark frames, and metallic hinges, used as a decorative room divider or privacy curtain.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thickness of the panels and a glimpse of the cherry blossom artwork at the top edge.
description_view_1: Frontal view displaying the full cherry blossom painting across all four panels, with hinges and frame details visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the plain, dark backs of the panels and the metallic hinges.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the unadorned, dark surfaces of all four panels and the hinge connections.
